# services.ghostunnel

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.ghostunnel`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.ghostunnel.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable ghostunnel. |
| `services.ghostunnel.package` | `package` | The ghostunnel package to use. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Server mode ghostunnels (TLS listener -> plain TCP/UNIX target) |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.allowAll` | `boolean` | If true, allow all clients, do not check client cert subject. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.allowCN` | `list of string` | Allow client if common name appears in the list. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.allowDNS` | `list of string` | Allow client if DNS subject alternative name appears in the list. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.allowOU` | `list of string` | Allow client if organizational unit name appears in the list. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.allowURI` | `list of string` | Allow client if URI subject alternative name appears in the list. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.cacert` | `null or string` | Path to CA bundle file (PEM/X509). Uses system trust store if `null`. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.cert` | `null or string` | Path to certificate (PEM with certificate chain). Not required if `keystore` is set. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.disableAuthentication` | `boolean` | Disable client authentication, no client certificate will be required. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.extraArguments` | `strings concatenated with " "` | Extra arguments to pass to `ghostunnel server` |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.key` | `null or string` | Path to certificate private key (PEM with private key). Not required if `keystore` is set. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.keystore` | `null or string` | Path to keystore (combined PEM with cert/key, or PKCS12 keystore). NB: storepass is not supported because it would expose credentials via `/proc/*/cmdline`. Specify this or `cert` and `key`. |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.listen` | `string` | Address and port to listen on (can be HOST:PORT, unix:PATH). |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.target` | `string` | Address to forward connections to (can be HOST:PORT or unix:PATH). |
| `services.ghostunnel.servers.<name>.unsafeTarget` | `boolean` | If set, does not limit target to localhost, 127.0.0.1, [::1], or UNIX sockets. This is meant to protect against accidental unencrypted traffic on untrusted networks. |
